triggers.name: Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5: Storage version has been changed
triggers.expression: last(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/huawei.5300.v5[version],#1)<>last(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/huawei.5300.v5[version],#2) and length(last(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/huawei.5300.v5[version]))>0
triggers.description: OceanStor 5300 V5 version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5: Unavailable by ICMP ping
triggers.description: Last three attempts returned timeout. Please check device connectivity.

triggers.name: Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5: High ICMP ping loss
triggers.expression: min(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$ICMP_LOSS_WARN} and min(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100
triggers.description: ICMP packets loss detected.

triggers.name: Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5: High ICMP ping response time
triggers.description: Average ICMP response time is too high.

triggers.name: Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5: System name has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/system.name,#1)<>last(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/system.name,#2) and length(last(/Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5 by SNMP/system.name))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Huawei OceanStor 5300 V5: No SNMP data collection
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

macros.description: The critical threshold of the CPU utilization expressed in %.
The time during which temperature of disk may exceed the threshold.
Maximum temperature of disk. Can be used with {#MODEL} as context.
The time during which average I/O response time of LUN may exceed the threshold.
Maximum average I/O response time of LUN in milliseconds.
The time during which memory usage may exceed the threshold.
Maximum percentage of memory used
The time during which average I/O latency of node may exceed the threshold.
Maximum average I/O latency of node in milliseconds.
The time during which free capacity may exceed the {#THRESHOLD} from hwInfoStoragePoolFullThreshold.
The time during which temperature of enclosure may exceed the threshold.
Maximum temperature of enclosure
Warning threshold of ICMP packet loss in %.
Warning threshold of the average ICMP response time in seconds.
Time interval for the SNMP availability trigger.

triggers.name: NetApp AFF A700: Cluster status is abnormal
triggers.description: Any errors associated with the sample. For example, if the aggregation of data over multiple nodes fails then any of the partial errors might be returned, “ok” on success, or “error” on any internal uncategorized failure. Whenever a sample collection is missed but done at a later time, it is back filled to the previous 15 second timestamp and tagged with "backfilled_data". “Inconsistent_ delta_time” is encountered when the time between two collections is not the same for all nodes. Therefore, the aggregated value might be over or under inflated. “Negative_delta” is returned when an expected monotonically increasing value has decreased in value. “Inconsistent_old_data” is returned when one or more nodes do not have the latest data.

triggers.name: NetApp AFF A700: Version has changed
triggers.expression: last(/NetApp AFF A700 by HTTP/netapp.cluster.version,#1)<>last(/NetApp AFF A700 by HTTP/netapp.cluster.version,#2) and length(last(/NetApp AFF A700 by HTTP/netapp.cluster.version))>0
triggers.description: The NetApp AFF A700 version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

macros.description: The HTTP agent timeout to wait for a response from AFF700.
AFF700 user password.
AFF700 cluster URL address.
AFF700 user name.

macros.description: The critical threshold of the CPU utilization expressed in %.
Minimum available space on the disk. Can be used with {#FSNAME} as context.
This macro is used in filesystems discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
This macro is used in filesystems discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
Maximum percentage of disk used. Can be used with {#FSNAME} as context.
The time during which disk usage may exceed the threshold. Can be used with {#FSNAME} as context.

This macro is used in filesystems discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
Value should be integer:
  2 - flexibleVolume,
  3 - aggregate,
  4 - stripedAggregate,
  5 - stripedVolume.

This macro is used in filesystems discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
Value should be integer:
  2 - flexibleVolume,
  3 - aggregate,
  4 - stripedAggregate,
  5 - stripedVolume.

Macro define what threshold will be used for disk space trigger:
  0 - use Bytes ({$FAS3220.FS.AVAIL.MIN.CRIT})
  1 - use percents ({$FAS3220.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT})
Can be used with {#FSNAME} as context.

  1 - cluster

  4 - intercluster
  5 - cluster-mgmt

triggers.description: Phone Home connects to the Pure1 service and uploads logs for continuous health monitoring.

triggers.name: Pure Storage FlashArray: RemoteAssist has been enabled
triggers.description: Purity's administrator-controlled RemoteAssist feature enables a Technical Support Engineer to communicate directly with the FlashArray via a secure link, effectively establishing an additional administrative session for the duration of the diagnosis and service.

triggers.description: An error occurred when trying to get volume data from the Pure Storage FlashArray API.
